sources_used: WebSearch (Google) for [metro] family-owned glass company / commercial glazier / shower door installer queries
Per-business WebFetch of homepage / About / history pages for verification of legal name, address, family-owned status, and services
Cross-reference of search-result excerpts when WebFetch returned 403/timeout/cert errors
sources_blocked: lubbockglass.com returned HTTP 403 (Lubbock Glass excluded — could not directly verify ownership)
tcglassmirror.com TLS cert hostname mismatch (Triple Crown Glass listed but address/phone unverified — excluded)
centuryglassdallas.com cert expired (Century Glass excluded — Dallas; founded 1945, family-owned per search excerpt but no direct fetch)
bedfordglass.net ECONNREFUSED (Bedford Glass & Mirror listed from search-result text; founded 1962 / family-owned per BBB)
sonoraglasstx.com WebFetch returned empty content (Sonora Glass not included)
proformance1.com cert expired (Pro-Formance Glass not included)
houtexglass.com first-cert error (Hou-Tex Glass not directly verified — excluded)
myaffordableglass.com timed out twice (My Affordable Glass not included)
hardwarespecialties.net / hensalglass.com partial verification (hardware specialties verified by 2 sources; hensalglass timed out so only included if independent confirmation existed)
Several Squarespace/Wix small-shop domains returned 403 to WebFetch; only included if business details were independently confirmable from multiple search-result excerpts
geographies_covered: Tarrant (Fort Worth, Benbrook, Bedford, Hurst, Mansfield)
Bell (Belton/Killeen — Bob's Screen + Triple Crown excluded due to cert errors)
exclusion_brands_observed_and_excluded: Glass Doctor (Neighborly franchise) — saw Glass Doctor of Lubbock, Glass Doctor of Texoma, Glass Doctor of Fort Worth in results, all excluded
Binswanger Glass — explicitly excluded (national multi-state operator with corporate ownership history)
The Glass Guru — excluded (national franchise network)
ambiguous_cases_flagged: Southern Glass & Mirror (Plano) — division of Novak Corporation. Not independent. EXCLUDED.
Lakeside Glass Houston — partners with Tristar Glass and Kawneer; family ownership not confirmed. INCLUDED with notes (commercial-glazing focused, 1987-founded).
Contractors Glass Products (Corpus Christi) — email domain @arrow-companies.com suggests possible Arrow Companies affiliation. INCLUDED with flag.

East Texas Glass Co and Henderson Glass Co — both list the SAME Kilgore street address (1710 State Highway 31 E) and both reference 'Arrow Glass Industries' / @arrow-companies.com email. Likely sister brands or recently rolled up. INCLUDED separately with cross-reference notes.
Denison Glass — page references 'Installed Building Products' in disclaimer. Possible IBP-affiliated. INCLUDED with flag.

Texan Glass & Solar Control — multiple Texas locations (Houston, Conroe, Cypress, Katy, Spring, Woodlands, Corpus Christi, Fort Worth). 25-year-old operator, family-owned per third-party reporting. INCLUDED.
Snyder Commercial Glass (Austin) — also covers Houston + San Antonio. Family + veteran owned per site. INCLUDED.
Shower Doors Today LLC (San Antonio) — founded 2016, only 9 years tenure; flagged for tenure threshold.
_meta.verification_methodology: Each row was confirmed by direct WebFetch of the business website or About page, capturing legal name, address, founded year, ownership type, and chain affiliation. Where WebFetch failed, businesses were only included if at least two independent search-result excerpts (chamber of commerce, BBB, third-party trade publication, etc.) confirmed family ownership, location, and founding year. Service mix was set based on services explicitly listed on the company website. Auto-glass-only shops (e.g., Scott Glass auto-only line) were classified as 'auto-glass' and de-prioritized. Independence was verified by absence of Belron/Safelite, Speedy Glass, Glass Doctor / Neighborly, Binswanger, Apogee, Glass Guru, and Gatsby branding in About / footer copy. Founded_year was only captured where stated by the business itself or by a verifiable third-party source (Chamber of Commerce / BBB / local newspaper). employee count was left null in all rows because no public source consistently disclosed staffing — that requires the enrichment phase, not spine.
